title: Create src/utils/logger.js with info, warn, error exports
description: Implement the stateless ESM logger module at src/utils/logger.js. Export three named functions — info(), warn(), error() — each accepting (message, context = {}). Each function must: (1) capture new Date().toISOString() as timestamp, (2) build a prefix [timestamp] [LEVEL], (3) call the matching console method with the prefix and message, appending context only when it has own enumerable keys. Follows the export function pattern from src/index.js. No dependencies, no default export.
acceptance_criteria: src/utils/logger.js exists and uses ESM named exports (no default export)
Importing and calling info('msg') logs a line starting with an ISO 8601 timestamp followed by [INFO]
Calling error('msg', { code: 42 }) passes the context object as a third argument to console.error
Calling warn('msg') with no context argument does NOT pass a trailing empty object to console.warn
No new packages appear in package.json after creating this file

title: Update package.json test script to run node:test
description: Replace the placeholder test script in package.json with node --test 'src/**/*.test.js' so that npm test executes the built-in Node test runner against all *.test.js files under src/. This unblocks task-003 and task-004 without adding any new dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: package.json scripts.test equals "node --test 'src/**/*.test.js'" or an equivalent glob that matches src/utils/logger.test.js
npm test exits non-zero only because no test files exist yet (not due to a missing or broken script)
No new entries appear in dependencies or devDependencies

title: Create src/utils/logger.test.js with unit tests for all three functions
description: Write a test file at src/utils/logger.test.js using node:test and node:assert/strict. Cover all three exported functions (info, warn, error) with three scenarios each: message only, message + non-empty context, and message + explicit null context (must not crash). Use console method spying via a mock or by temporarily replacing console.info/warn/error to assert the correct arguments are passed.
acceptance_criteria: File imports from 'node:test' and 'node:assert/strict' only — no third-party test libs
Tests assert that the first argument to the underlying console method starts with a string matching /^\[\d{4}-.*\] \[(INFO|WARN|ERROR)\]/
Tests assert context is passed when provided and omitted when not provided
Tests assert that passing null as context does not throw
All tests in the file are syntactically valid and discoverable by node --test

title: Run npm test and confirm all tests pass (exit 0 gate)
description: Execute npm test to run the full node:test suite and verify it exits 0. This is the cycle gate: all prior tasks must be complete before this task starts. Fix any failures discovered during the run before marking this task done.
acceptance_criteria: Output shows 3 passing tests (one per exported function, or more if sub-cases are split)
No test is marked as skipped or failing
No changes to source files are required after this step (implementation is stable)
